## Forum Threads [...] Hareesh Vemasani
ProductBridge

ProductBridge

@olia\_nemirovski Great example — and yes, exactly the kind of case our dedup is built for. “It’s slow” and “keeps timing out” share zero common words but describe the same underlying problem. Our RAG + LLM matches by intent, not wording — so those two get grouped correctly.

Gabe Moronta
Visla

Visla

@hareesh\_vemasani Congrats on this lunch, wish you well!

Rifat OKTAR

@hareesh\_vemasani congrats. I've tried. According to my first look, very usefull.

Davit Svanidze
FlowMarket

FlowMarket [...] On prioritization: we don't just count votes. Every user in ProductBridge can be tagged with properties — like the MRR they bring in, their plan type, or any custom attribute. So when feedback comes in, you're not just seeing how many people asked — you're seeing the weight behind who asked. A request from 3 high-MRR customers can and should outrank 20 requests from free users.

On dedup across channels: we use advanced RAG + LLM, so matching happens at the intent level, not keyword level. And the AI already knows your full context — knowledge base, existing feedback, roadmap, and changelog. So the same problem phrased differently across Slack, Intercom, and email gets grouped correctly.

Saul Fleischman
RiteKit Company Logo API

RiteKit Company Logo API

results.title: The best geo tools in 2026 | Product Hunt
results.content:
Today, we wanted to look at AI creative tools from a slightly different angle not just features, but the real-world marketing activity behind them.

### Engineering & Development

### LLMs

### Productivity

### Marketing & Sales

### Design & Creative

### Social & Community

### Finance

### AI Agents

### Trending categories

### Top reviewed

### Trending products

### Top forum threads [...] KIVA (By Wellows)

A year ago, half the marketing world told us "AI search" was overhyped. The other half was shipping "ChatGPT SEO checklists" in a week.

We ignored both.

Instead we did one boring thing: we scraped LLM citations. Every day. Across ChatGPT, Perplexity, Gemini, Claude. For hundreds of brands. And we asked one question when AI recommends a product, where does that recommendation actually come from?

Here's what we found that nobody was talking about:

Sebastián Álvarez
Product Hunt

Product Hunt

Everyone is hyping up GEO as the new SEO. Honestly? They re just merging.   
But hype aside, if you aren't tracking how ChatGPT or Perplexity cite your product, you're flying blind.   
What tools are you actually using to monitor your brand's AI visibility right now? [...] Ashish Mishra

Thirdeye provides AI-search analytics for performance oriented marketing teams. Unlike classic search engine tools that chase organic rankings, it focuses on how large language models (LLMs) such as ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini and Perplexity mention and cite brands.

The platform monitors brand mentions across these models in real time, analyses sentiment and provides prompts showing how and where the brand is being referenced. It also sends real time alerts when a brand is cited by an AI model and offers competitor monitoring to identify positioning gaps.

Janice
AdFox (formerly GoodsFox)

AdFox (formerly GoodsFox)

Product Hunt is home to amazing products across every category.
